issue_title: 'Show version information' is not working
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

The CLI does not appear to be returning the version, when the associated arg is invoked. Quoting supported args:

```
docker run quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest --help
Options:
-V, --version        Show version information
```

Whenever running `docker run quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest --version` or 'docker run quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest -V` an exception is thrown.

Details below.



### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

Container should output version information and exit gracefully

### Actual behavior

```bash
docker run quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest --version
The DelayedHandler was closed before any children handlers were configured. Messages will be written to stderr.
2023-10-06 11:09:42,128 DEBUG [org.jboss.logging] (main) Logging Provider: org.jboss.logging.JBossLogManagerProvider

Exception in thread "main" java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:77)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568)
	at io.quarkus.bootstrap.runner.QuarkusEntryPoint.doRun(QuarkusEntryPoint.java:61)
	at io.quarkus.bootstrap.runner.QuarkusEntryPoint.main(QuarkusEntryPoint.java:32)
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "picocli.CommandLine.getCommand()" because "cmdCommand" is null
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.Picocli.hasConfigChanges(Picocli.java:260)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.Picocli.requiresReAugmentation(Picocli.java:141)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.Picocli.runReAugmentationIfNeeded(Picocli.java:126)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.Picocli.parseAndRun(Picocli.java:95)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.KeycloakMain.main(KeycloakMain.java:88)
	... 6 more
```

### How to Reproduce?

docker run quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest --version

### Anything else?

_No response_

fix: allows --version to work (#24161)

closes #23783

issue_title: Can not create attribute group before setting/removing an annotation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When creating an attribute group, there is a single empty annotation in the `Annotations` section.

If you don't remove this "default" empty annotation you can create a group.

Annotations should not be mandatory when creating groups, similar to when you are creating attributes.

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

Create a group without being forced to add an annotation but just the group name.

### Actual behavior

Forced to delete the empty annotation or set values to it.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a attribute group
* Set a name
* Save

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not show empty annotation when creating attribute group (#23922)

Closes #23789

issue_title: The `Enabled When` setting should be set to `Always` by default
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When you try to create an attribute by providing only the `Name` and right after you go to the edit attribute page of the same attribute, the values from the following fields do not match with the values shown when creating the attribute:

* `Enabled when`

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

The default value in the create attribute page set to `Always`

### Actual behavior

The default value in the create attribute page is not set to `Always`

### How to Reproduce?

* Create an attribute
* Set a name
* Save
* Edit the attribute
* Compare how the `Enabled when` was when creating the attribute and now

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Enhance user profile attribute form UX (#24083)

Enhances the user experience for the create and edit form for User Profile attribute by making the following changes:

- Prevents the scopes from being persisted if an attribute can always be shown.
- Hides the scopes if 'Always' is selected, as the control is not interactive.
- Sets the attribute to be editable by admins by default.

Closes #24081
Closes #23790

Co-authored-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: New admin console doesn't support automatic logout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Scenario like:
- Login to admin console https://localhost:8443/admin/master/console/ in tab1
- Open admin console also in another browser tab (tab2). I should be logged automatically due the SSO and it works this way.
- Click "Sign out" in tab2. I am logged-out, which is OK
- Go to tab1. I am still logged-in here, which is not OK. When I click to some link (For instance tab "Clients" on the left), I am redirected to login screen as SSO session was already removed, which is OK


### Version

22.0.4 (or latest Keycloak main)

### Expected behavior

Automatic logout in tab1 due the session iframe after user logged-out in tab2. 

### Actual behavior

Not being logged-out automatically in tab1 until I click on some link

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

Not 100% sure if this can be strictly considered a bug or missing functionlity (feature). On the other hand, this worked in the old admin console and hence I consider it as a bug and also as a regression, so adding regression label here.
commit_message:
Automatically re-authenticate on single-logout (#28723)

Automatically forces the user to re-authenticate from the Admin and Account consoles when a single-logout occurs.

Closes #23832
Closes #23833

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Jon Koops <jonkoops@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Jon Koops <jonkoops@gmail.com>

issue_title: Account console v2 doesn't support automatic logout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

Scenario like:

- Login to account console https://localhost:8443/admin/master/console/ in tab1. I did not enabled any preview features etc, so assume it is account console V2.
- Open account console also in another browser tab (tab2). I should be logged automatically due the SSO and it works this way.
- Click "Sign out" in tab2. I am logged-out, which is OK
- Go to tab1. I am still logged-in here, which is not OK. When I click to some link (For instance tab "Personal info" on the left), I am redirected to login screen as SSO session was already removed, which is OK


### Version

22.0.4 (or latest Keycloak main)

### Expected behavior

Automatic logout in tab1 due the session iframe after user logged-out in tab2.

### Actual behavior

Not being logged-out automatically in tab1 until I click on some link

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

Not 100% sure if this can be strictly considered a bug or missing functionality (feature). Also it is not a regression since old account console (Account console V1) was not based on javascript and automatic logout did not work here. So feel free to convert to "Feature" or "Enhancement".
commit_message:
Automatically re-authenticate on single-logout (#28723)

Automatically forces the user to re-authenticate from the Admin and Account consoles when a single-logout occurs.

issue_title: Attempt to request storage access in Firefox
issue_body:
As part of fixing #22839 the choice was made to keep the implementation simple and not to call [`requestStorageAccess()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Document/requestStorageAccess) due to  the added unpredictable behavior.

Under this issue the following must be done:

- Add the [`allow-storage-access-by-user-activation`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Storage_Access_API#security_measures)  option to the [`sandbox`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/iframe#sandbox) attribute of the iframes embedded by Keycloak JS
- Implement the rest of the steps as outlined by ["Checking and requesting storage access"](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Storage_Access_API/Using#checking_and_requesting_storage_access).
- Verify this improves compatibility as per [the test matrix](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/23393#issuecomment-1727784845).

issue_body:

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

If the attribute name in LDAP (openldap is used) has a case error when defining a mapping in a user federation the result is that when editing the attributes of a user new attributes can be CREATED from that mapping but CANNOT BE EDITED. 

### Version

The behavior should be consistent. If case matching is required in the LDAP attribute name it should no longer work when creating the attribute. Conversely, if case is not required, updating an existing value for that attribute should work.

The attributes of a user new attributes can be CREATED from that mapping but CANNOT BE EDITED. 

### How to Reproduce?

Modify any existing and functional mapping by introducing some case difference. You will then be able to create values for this attribute but you will not be able to modify or delete them.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Session/EntityManager is closed when using infinispan and attempting to update the realm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

This bug most readily presents itself when attempting to use the session revocation feature in the admin UI. 

Only happens when using infinispan, any request to update the realm fails. 

### Version

Nightly

### Expected behavior

The realm should be able to update and the hibernate entity manager should not be closed for the current session

### Actual behavior

Error returns, request fails, realm does not update, error message says that the entity manager is closed

### How to Reproduce?

This bug most readily presents itself when attempting to use the session revocation feature in the admin UI. 

Run keycloak using legacy store and infinispan.
Login to the admin console, navigate to sessions. In the top right corner click the dropdown and select "Revocation". Select "Set To Now". The request will fail. This is also seen in GHA. In the session_spec for UI tests in cypress there is a test that does exactly this and fails due to this bug. 

While the revocation feature is no longer needed according to other discussion threads, this bug is actually caused by behavior in an unrelated class when updating a realm and most likely affects other areas of the UI. 

### Anything else?

I have diagnosed this issue and will be submitting a PR to resolve. It is caused by persistence of a cached realm model on OAuth2DeviceConfig in infinispan, causing a reference to an old session and entity manager instead of the most up to date
commit_message: Remove realm model storage from OAuth2DeviceConfig class to avoid persisting old session and entity manager in infinispan fixes keycloak/keycloak#23943

issue_title: Group members are displayed incorrectly when using LDAP in READ_ONLY mode
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When user federation via LDAP is configured and the group mapper is set to READ_ONLY mode, group members are sometimes duplicated in the admin UI. More specifically, the same group members are listed multiple times on different pages in the admin UI. This does not appear to be a UI bug though as the API calls actually return the same users for different pages.

Making a request to the admin API `/admin/realms/{realm}/groups/{id}/members` manually shows the same behaviour when requesting group members page by page (using the `first` and `max` parameters). Requesting group members with `max` set to a higher value than the group has members returns all members correctly without duplicates.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The admin API returns group members correctly without any duplicates.

### Actual behavior

The admin API endpoint `/admin/realms/{realm}/groups/{id}/members` returns the same users multiple times on different pages when using pagination.

### How to Reproduce?

- Setup LDAP user federation with the group mapper set to READ_ONLY mode.
- Create a LDAP group with more than 100 members (the default page size in the admin UI).
- Query the group members in Keycloak.

### Anything else?

The actual pagination happens in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/membership/MembershipType.java#L87. Multiple sequential requests will not keep some kind of cursor for pagination, so all group members are queried with every request. The order of the returned users is not defined anywhere and as a `Set<LDAPDn>` is used for the query results, the iteration order is not necessarily defined. A fix would be to sort the returned users before getting a sublist as proposed in #23784.

issue_title: Wrong value for VALIDATED_ID_TOKEN stored in the brokered identity context for external token exchange
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

This bug was reported as https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/8833, but seems to have been fixed in a bad manner which causes any custom mappers having to do an extra type check for the `VALIDATED_ID_TOKEN` data in the brokered identity context.

The issue is triggered when an external token exchange has `subject_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:id_token`. Then the brokered identity context's `VALIDATED_ID_TOKEN` data field is set to the raw `subject_token` string instead of the parsed `JsonWebToken` object. This will later make mappers relying on `VALIDATED_ID_TOKEN` being a `JsonWebToken` unable to get claims from the validated ID token used for the token exchange without parsing the JWT once again.
This was fixed by commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/7b58783255fb4059c125b168c7ec0bd68cb17778, but this commit only fixes it for mappers and code which uses the `getClaimValue` mapper in the `AbstractClaimMapper`.

Instead I propose we fix the actual bug, which is that the wrong object is set to the context data in the first place. This makes it easier to write custom and specialized mappers.

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

`brokeredIdentityContext.getContextData().get(VALIDATED_ID_TOKEN)` is expected to always return a `JsonWebToken` instance.

### Actual behavior

`brokeredIdentityContext.getContextData().get(VALIDATED_ID_TOKEN)` returns a String representing the `subject_token` in the token exchange.

### How to Reproduce?

Do an external token exchange with a custom mapper which doesn't use the `org.keycloak.broker.oidc.mappers.AbstractClaimMapper#getClaimValue` method.

### Anything else?

See
1. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/8833
2. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/7b58783255fb4059c125b168c7ec0bd68cb17778

issue_title: Operator operand env var does not conform to OSBS naming requirements
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

OpenShift Build Service has requirements for recognizing image coordinates for digest-pinning in a CSV. Particularly, any image coordinate specified as an environment variable for the operator container must have a variable name beginning with `RELATED_IMAGE_`

The operator code uses the name `OPERATOR_KEYCLOAK_IMAGE`, which is incompatible with this requirement. This leads to digest pinning not working when building a operator bundle via OSBS.

https://osbs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/users.html#pullspec-locations

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The image coordinate should be pinned by digest at build time by OSBS

### Actual behavior

The image coordinate is ignored by OSBS, remaining unpinned

### How to Reproduce?

Submit a OSBS build with operator bundle mode and digest pinning configured

### Anything else?

Since this would represent a breaking change, it may be best to accept both forms of the environment variable
commit_message:
Optionally accept RELATED_IMAGE_KEYCLOAK as the operand coordinate (#24018)

This changes the quarkus configuration of the operator so that if the
RELATED_IMAGE_KEYCLOAK environment variable is present, it will be
accepted over the default value

Closes #24017

issue_title: User profile tweaks in registration forms
issue_body:
### Description

There are some minor issues with user profile on registration forms:

- The fields `password` and `password-confirm` are still sent to user-profile methods when validating/creating/updating user. They are fortunately filtered here, but will be better to avoid sending them to user-profile methods at all (security hardening). This is follow-up on commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/5958c794#diff-f5907ffc7a2cbd0b6618943e1d8b71e85ee5ebaebc5c107f4660954ef5651489 (I recall we discussed to avoid sending passwords to user-profile as a follow-up to this task)

- In `RegisterUserCreation` and `RegisterProfile`, the user-profile instance is always created 2 times per request (once in method `validate()` and second in method `success()`. This causes that `success()` method has new user-profile instance and hence needs to also call `validate()` method another time (due this https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.4/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultUserProfile.java#L83-L85 and this https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.4/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultUserProfile.java#L94-L96 ). I've checked that when there is single instance per `RegisterUserCreation` and single per `RegisterProfile` means 3 (or more) SQL queries due to avoid repeating same validation.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Details

issue_title: Lots of parameters related to identity brokering uses `providerId` when they expect `providerAlias`
issue_body:
### Description

Snippets from related discussion:

Hi team, just reaching out to discuss something that confused us at first. We are using some of the methods in org.keycloak.services.Urls - more specifically #identityProviderAuthnRequest - and the providerId param caught us off-guard since IdPs do have a providerId value (my understanding is this refers to the type of provider that the IdP is based off of e.g. keycloak-oidc-improved), but it seems that this method (and perhaps other methods in the class) are actually looking for the alias of an IdP. This became more apparent when reviewing how the method was used by other places in the code, which is usually a good thing to check. However by just looking at the method itself it does seem a bit easy to get confused. It does make more sense that the alias is used here imo since when building a url it is the alias that is used and i am not sure there would be much value in having the providerId in the url.

I was thinking about opening up a PR to have this discussion and would be happy to do that if you would like

@Andrew Rhine Good catch. I am looking at IdentityBrokerService.performAccount and it expects alias (not provider_id). So it is just bad name of parameter...

PR is welcome to fix it 🙂 Both IdentityBrokerService and Urls and maybe other classes (if affected, but probably not...)

issue_title: Scopes still persisted when required attribute is set to `Always`
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When creating or updating an attribute, setting the `Required when` setting to `Always` still persists the scopes from the `Scopes are requested` option.

The `Always` and `Scopes are requested` are mutually exclusive.

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

The Admin UI should work as follows:

* If the `Enabled when` setting is set to `Always`, the `selector` JSON field of the attribute configuration should not be set at all.
* If the `Required when` setting is set to `Always`, the `required.scopes` JSON field of the attribute configuration should not be set at all.

### Actual behavior

The client scopes are always set when enabling or marking a feature as required even though the `Always` option is set.

### How to Reproduce?

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20852#issuecomment-1581093287.

### Anything else?

Created from https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20852#issuecomment-1581093287.

issue_title: Handle "You are already logged in" for expired authentication sessions
issue_body:
### Description

Follow-up on #12406 :

It can still happen that the message "You are already logged-in" would be displayed to the user even with #12406 . For instance with this scenario:
1) User has Chrome browser with set `On startup` option to `Continue where you left off` (See https://www.microcenter.com/tech_center/article/8051/how-to-continue-where-you-left-off-in-the-chrome-browser) . This means that when the Chrome browser is (re)started, all browser tabs are refreshed eagerly and they automatically send the HTTP request(s) to web pages, which were opened last time (no need to click on the tabs to refresh automatically) 
2) User is logged in some Keycloak applications (EG. JIRA) in 2 browser tabs. He has also another (unrelated) browser tab opened with some random web page
3) User restarts his Chrome browser
4) ATM the 2 browser tabs with JIRA are automatically refreshed and send requests to Keycloak and both would be redirected to the login screen. But user has focus on other browser tab with "Random web page". So at this moment, there is `RootAuthenticationSession` on the server with 2 tabs (authentication sessions) attached to it
5) User does some clicking on web (or switch to other applications) for 1 hour. 
6) Then he returns back to some of the JIRA tabs. He tries to login here, but see the message like `You took too long to login`. due authenticationSession is already expired. Authentication session is restarted now (due `KC_RESTART` cookie) and user can login in tab1
7) After login in tab1, the other tab tab2 is not authenticated. The `authChecker.js` javascript won't be able to authenticate user here because it belongs to the previous expired authentication session. 
8) When user go to tab2 now and attempt to finish login, he would see message "You are already logged in"

## Possible solution

Introduce parameters like `redirect_uri` to the endpoints of `LoginActionsService`. This means that instead of display `You are already logged-in`, we will be able to redirect user to the `redirect_uri`. We can add parameters like `error` and `state` to indicate that there was an error on the server due expired session to signal that client should try to re-send the request. We might need to introduce parameters like `state` or `response_mode` as the `state` should be present in error response and we need to know which `response_mode` to use.

So in case of OIDC client, we can send back error response to the redirectUri with given `state` as described in OAuth2/OIDC specifications and with the usage of correct response mode.

Hopefully this can be re-used for SAML clients as well (The `state` parameter from LoginActionsService can be transferred to `RelayState` in the SAML error response. Also `response_mode` parameter of `LoginActionsService` can be hopefully used to figure what SAML binding to use.

This might need some additions to `LoginProtocol` interface for how to send the error for this case (not yet 100% sure...)

For the 1st iteration, we may not need this behaviour to be configurable, but we can possible update it afterwards if needed.

issue_title: HTTP/2 - Last parameter of POST form data contains 0x00 byte in some configurations.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

This is a different manifestation of a previous issue with HTTP/2 handling and empty dataframes - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/13315

When an HTTP/2 capable client sends POST data to a Keycloak instance running the current nightly codebase and also ends the HTTP/2 stream with an empty dataframe, the last parameter in the POST data will have a `0x00` byte appended to it. 

This current issue will show up in odd ways - such as Keycloak responding that `grant_type=client_credentials` is an invalid grant type when used at the OIDC Token endpoint, or that PKCE values are invalid.

This is ultimately a problem that should be handled by the resteasy-reactive implementation, but logging this bug for tracking purposes within Keycloak itself.

### Version

main / nightly

### Expected behavior

No `0x00` byte is appending to any form parameters.

### Actual behavior

`0x00` byte is appended to last form parameter, breaking various flows and making the deployment unusable.

### How to Reproduce?


Background:
Our deployment makes use of Openshift, and that uses HAProxy configured to reencrypt and send data to the backend (Keycloak) using http/2 - the HAProxy implementation uses an empty dataframe to close the stream.

Actually reproducing it:
Configure Keycloak with HTTPS and an appropriate TLS certificate.
Use the reproducer code from https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/13315#issuecomment-1238125070
Debug the code in Keycloak and notice that the last form parameter contains an additional `0x00`

Alternatively, without using Keycloak, it can be reproduced using a generic quarkus app + some of the constructs Keycloak uses

See - https://github.com/jsorah/reproducer/tree/null_bytes_appended_to_form_data

### Anything else?

Disabling HTTP/2 will prevent this issue from occurring.
